Notice And Motion For Order Of Foreclosure On Attys' Lien
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Harold P. Smith of Dhillon & Smith LLP (433.1700) for plaintiff and moving party; Gregory J. Brod of the Brod Law Firm (397.1130) for defendant and responding party

Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF DHILLON & SMITH, LLP'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF FORECLOSURE ON ATTORNEYS' LIEN is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff cites no authority that an action to foreclose on an attorney's lien can be resolved on the merits by a motion of this kind. Indeed, plaintiff's moving papers appear not to include a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the motion, which the court construes as an admission the motion lacks merit. CRC 3.1113(a). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
